danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile creatures in level spaces
danger_signals.meaning: Collision with enemies results in energy loss; spatial threat indicator
Player damage state; proximity to game-over condition
Level failure imminent if time expires

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen edges, pits, bottomless spaces (unless Solid Bottom Mode enabled)
danger_signals.meaning: Level geometry that causes instant death or energy loss

safety_signals.meaning: Progress indicator; completing gem collection advances to next level
Optional progression opportunity; unlocks bonus stage without Shovel requirement

safety_signals.visual_form: Energy recovery items (inferred from energy loss mechanic)
safety_signals.meaning: Mitigates danger state; extends survival duration

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Jungle antagonists (animals, environmental threats from source material)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Level spaces designed as jungle environments; platform layout represents natural terrain

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Diverse creature designs reflecting Disney Jungle Book cast (presumed: Shere Khan, monkeys, snakes)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Animal-themed sprite designs; environmental placement in jungle biomes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gems remain visible in level; collectible counter not full
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol or move in predictable patterns; level remains playable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All gems collected; level completion triggered
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Transition to next level or bonus stage (if Shovel found)
Automatic level failure; return to level start or game over
Player death; loss of life or game over state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shovel item visible in level; visual distinction from gems
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Collecting Shovel triggers bonus stage entry after level completion

environmental_storytelling.example: Jungle environment composed of platforms, vegetation, and animal-themed obstacles communicating the Disney film's setting
Gems as primary progression currency; Shovel as secondary optional objective — visual distinction signals different reward tiers
Platform arrangement creates navigational challenge; enemy placement guides player movement patterns and communicates safe/unsafe zones

environmental_storytelling.visual_manifestation: Energy meter, timer, gem counter displayed simultaneously
environmental_storytelling.meaning: Communicates multiple world states (health, time pressure, progress) in single glance

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Move in predictable patterns; spatial positioning indicates safe approach angles
Collision detection triggers energy loss; no special attack animation documented
Energy meter decreases; visual knockback or state change (inferred from platformer conventions)
Stationary; visual clarity indicates pickupability; collection advances progress counter
Stationary; collision triggers bonus level access; optional progression path

notes: Source material provides limited direct documentation of visual manifestation systems. Analysis inferred from platformer genre conventions (NES era) and Disney Jungle Book IP context. Game features hidden debug menu enabling world-state manipulation (Solid Bottom Mode, No Energy Loss, Infinite Lives/Time, Single Gem Mode, Force Bonus Level) — these options suggest underlying systems for energy management, gem collection tracking, level completion conditions, and bonus stage gating. No specific color palette, faction symbols, or detailed enemy behavior descriptions available in provided source.
